channel.title: The Quietus – Album of the Week
channel.description: Album of the Week reviews from The Quietus.

item.title: Nature Boy: Driving Through Belgium by Anton Pearson
item.description: This ambient side project from the guitarist in Squid reaches back through a deep history of British music inspired by the countryside

item.title: Plastic Alphabet: Actual Earth Music – Volume 3 & 4 by EarthBall
item.description: Exploratory Canadians capture lightning in a bottle, marshalling their transcendent free rock into a package that feels joyful, communicating effortlessly

item.title: Quiet Chaos : A World of Bodies on Fire by Tony Njoku
item.description: Eschewing his former maximalist approach to music making, Tony Njoku’s new EP sounds like a complete reset, where he rediscovers his voice and lets go of frustration through meditations for synth and piano

item.title: Society of the Spectacles: Demand to Be Taken to Heaven Alive! by Horse Lords
item.description: Baltimore avant-rock group propose a revolution in everyday life, reconstructing American vernacular traditions from the ground up

item.title: What Lasts is Consciousness: All Gates Open – the Original Motion Picture Soundtrack by Blood Incantation
item.description: The soundtrack to a new film about the Denver heavy metal four-piece takes the listener deep into an exploration of outer – and inner – space

item.title: Reach for the Scars: Cicatrizes Do Futuro by Deafkids
item.description: Drawing inspiration from the carnival-esque rituals of Bumba Meu Boi, the Brazilian duo find paths to the future in ancient rhythms, not far-off galaxies

item.title: In the Shadow of the Bomb: Heavy Water by Magic Tuber Stringband
item.description: Magic Tuber Stringband’s first LP as a trio is a work of unruly beauty that doubles as a cautionary tale, says Bernie Brooks

item.title: Shatterproof: Sam Hoyek’s Demonstration 01: Anomalous
item.description: An album of sonic and metaphysical skirmishes from the Syrian-born, Montreal-based experimental musician
